text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Amelia Hart 
Consultant Neurologist  
Riverside Neurology Clinic  
St Stephen’s Wing, Riverside NHS Trust, London  

Dear Dr Amelia Hart, 

Mr Daniel Price, DOB: 22-09-1975, Hospital No: RX84-2291 NHS No. NHS-8834201976  
Flat 4, 12 Willow Court, Brixton, London, SW2 1AB  

Diagnosis: 

Recurrent unprovoked seizures, currently with sustained absence of episodes over the last 18 months on treatment

Aetiology: 

No definitive structural or metabolic cause identified on MRI brain (2024) and routine bloods; ambulatory EEG (2024) non-diagnostic

Other Medical Problems: 

Bilateral presbycusis with intermittent tinnitus; uses over-the-ear amplification at home. Prefers scheduled speakerphone calls with a family member present to support communication.

Management Plan: 

- Continue sodium valproate 500 mg twice daily. Maintain the same brand and dosing schedule.  
- Continue vitamin D3 1000 IU once daily and check bone profile and vitamin D at next review.  
- Driving: patient remains within DVLA guidance given the prolonged absence of events; advised to inform DVLA and insurer as per standard requirements.  
- Safety: reinforce adherence, regular sleep, avoidance of missed doses, and moderation of alcohol intake.  
- Investigations: no further neuroimaging needed at present; will repeat trough valproate level and LFTs prior to next visit.  
- Follow-up: routine review in 9 months. We will arrange appointments as pre-booked speakerphone calls with a nominated family member present, as per the patient’s communication preference. 

Since our last review, there have been no witnessed or reported episodes suggestive of seizures. He describes feeling steady on his current regimen with no dose omissions and only mild, transient post-dose nausea in the mornings, which has not limited activities. He remains in full-time employment and has resumed swimming twice weekly without difficulty, adhering to standard water safety advice. Sleep has improved with a regular schedule. No reports of falls, injuries, or auras. Given this ongoing stability, I have not altered therapy.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: currently with sustained absence of episodes over the last 18 months on treatment

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states "sustained absence of episodes over the last 18 months" and also notes no witnessed or reported episodes since the last review. This confirms current seizure-free status and that it has persisted for at least 6 months. Therefore, normalize to 'seizure free for 18 month'.
seizure_frequency_number: currently with sustained absence of episodes over the last 18 months on treatment

flaws.description: Gold (seizure free for 18 month) disagrees with Reference (seizure free for multiple month)
